text: Answer FOUR of the ten essay questions.
Answer each question in a separate booklet.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer FOUR of the ten essays.
content: Discuss why neurotransmitter uptake transporters are important drug targets.
Compare and contrast stimulus localisation in somatic sensation and hearing.
What is meant by contrast within a visual scene? How might it arise? Why is contrast important for visual perception?
Charles Sherrington wrote that "a simple reflex is probably a purely abstract conception". To what extent does the organisation of the motor system support this view?

Describe the sequence of decisions that lead to the differentiation of a mammalian spinal motor neuron. Begin at the onset of gastrulation. Highlight similarities and differences with the development of a neuron in the Drosophila ventral nerve cord.
Critically discuss the mechanisms by which the specificity of synaptic connections is genetically encoded.
Describe how synaptic plasticity rules can change over the organism's lifespan supporting your essay with evidence from animal models.
What does the hypothalamus contribute to motivated behaviour?
Evaluate the claim that stages of language processing proceed independently from each other, using both neural and behavioural evidence.
What have accidental and intentional neural manipulations taught us about the function of the prefrontal cortex?
